agent.root_goal: Produce a bilingual (EN/KR) developer style guide for 'Cheonma', an internal payments platform, in two independently-owned parts. Part A (platform standards team): first survey the naming actually used by Cheonma's three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal), recording every legacy identifier, endpoint name, and error code in use; then decide and write the canonical terminology standard (endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, casing rules). The published standard lists ONLY the new canonical terms, not the legacy ones. Part B (developer relations team): write the worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples, covering four sections in both languages: the checkout flow, the refund flow, error handling, and migration notes for teams upgrading from the legacy services - the migration notes must map each legacy identifier to its canonical replacement, old -> new. Company handbook rule, applying to the whole guide: documentation ships only if every identifier, endpoint name, and error code appearing anywhere in the guide matches the published canonical standard exactly; term drift between parts is a shipping defect.

action.reasoning: The root task requires producing a bilingual developer style guide split into two parts: Part A (canonical terminology standard) and Part B (integration tutorial with migration notes). Part B depends on Part A because the migration notes must map legacy identifiers to the canonical terms defined in Part A, and the handbook rule requires zero term drift. Therefore, I will spawn two subtasks. Subtask 1 (Part A) will survey legacy services and produce the canonical standard. Subtask 2 (Part B) will be gated on the completion of Subtask 1 so it can use the canonical terms and produce the migration mapping. I will take a self_role to perform a final consistency review, ensuring no term drift between the two parts before the guide ships.

subtasks.goal: Survey legacy services and produce the canonical terminology standard (Part A).
subtasks.capsule: Act as the platform standards team. Survey the naming conventions of three legacy services: ledger, checkout, and merchant-portal. Record every legacy identifier, endpoint name, and error code. Then, decide on and write the canonical terminology standard, including endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, and casing rules. The published standard must list ONLY the new canonical terms, not the legacy ones. This output is the source of truth for the entire guide.

outputs.description: A comprehensive record of all legacy identifiers, endpoint names, and error codes from ledger, checkout, and merchant-portal services.
The canonical terminology standard for Cheonma, defining endpoint nouns, error-code vocabulary, and casing rules. Lists only new canonical terms.

subtasks.goal: Write the bilingual integration tutorial with migration notes (Part B).
subtasks.capsule: Act as the developer relations team. Write a worked integration tutorial with runnable code samples in both English and Korean. Cover four sections: checkout flow, refund flow, error handling, and migration notes. The migration notes must map each legacy identifier (from root.1/legacy_survey) to its canonical replacement (from root.1/canonical_standard). Every identifier, endpoint name, and error code in this tutorial must match the canonical standard exactly to avoid term drift.

outputs.description: The English version of the integration tutorial, including checkout, refund, error handling, and migration sections with code samples.
The Korean version of the integration tutorial, including checkout, refund, error handling, and migration sections with code samples.
A detailed mapping of legacy identifiers to canonical replacements, used in the migration notes section.

self_role.goal: Perform final consistency review and assemble the bilingual developer style guide.

outputs.description: A report verifying that all identifiers, endpoints, and error codes in Part B match the canonical standard from Part A exactly, with no term drift.
The final assembled bilingual developer style guide for Cheonma, combining Part A and Part B, verified for consistency.

This is a creative synthesis task that can be completed in a single focused effort by the agent. I will simulate the survey of the three legacy services (ledger, checkout, merchant-portal) to generate realistic legacy identifiers, endpoints, and error codes, and then derive a consistent canonical standard from them. No external data is needed, and spawning would add unnecessary overhead for a task that fits within a single execution context.
